name: Apple (Raw)
notes: Raw apples can increase Vata (gas/bloating). Best eaten cooked or spiced with cinnamon for Vata.

name: Apple (Cooked)
notes: Excellent for Vata. Stewed apples are a great Ayurvedic breakfast.

name: Banana (Ripe)
notes: Heavy and sticky. Good for Vata, but increases Kapha. Do not mix with milk (incompatible food).

name: Pomegranate
notes: Tridoshic. Excellent for balancing Pitta and cleansing blood.

name: Lemon
notes: Stimulates digestion. Good for Vata and Kapha, but aggravates Pitta in excess.

name: Broccoli
notes: Can cause gas for Vata. Must be cooked well with oil/ghee and spices (cumin, ginger).

name: Sweet Potato
notes: Grounding and nourishing. Excellent for Vata and Pitta.

name: Zucchini
notes: Very easy to digest, mild. Great for Pitta.

name: Basmati Rice
notes: Lightest of all rice. Tridoshic if cooked with a little ghee. Ideal staple.

name: Quinoa
notes: Light and dry. Great for Kapha, but Vata needs it cooked with plenty of moisture and oil.

name: Oats (Cooked)
notes: Moist and grounding. Avoid dry oats (like granola) for Vata.

name: Mung Dal (Split Yellow)
notes: The most digestible legume. Tridoshic. Base for Kitchari.

name: Chickpeas (Garbanzo)
notes: Very Vata-aggravating. Must be soaked overnight, cooked very soft with heavy spices and oil.

name: Cow's Ghee
notes: Best fat in Ayurveda. Rejuvenating (Rasayana), improves digestion (Agni) without aggravating Pitta.

name: Cow's Milk
notes: Must be boiled, consumed warm, and preferably spiced (cardamom/turmeric). Do not mix with fruit or salt.

name: Yogurt
notes: Heavy, channel-blocking (Abhishyandi). Avoid at night. Best consumed as watered-down buttermilk (Takra).

name: Almonds
notes: Must be soaked overnight and peeled. Sattvic and highly nourishing for brain and Vata.

name: Sesame Seeds
notes: Best seed for Vata dosha. Deeply nourishing and warming.

name: Turmeric
notes: Tridoshic in moderation. Anti-inflammatory, blood purifier.

name: Ginger (Fresh)
notes: Better for Pitta than dry ginger. Excellent for digestion and respiratory issues.

name: Ginger (Dry)
notes: Hotter and more penetrating than fresh ginger. Reduces Kapha and Vata.

name: Cumin
notes: Essential digestive spice. Enhances absorption.

name: Coriander Seeds
notes: Best spice for Pitta. Cools the digestive tract.

name: Fennel Seeds
notes: Excellent for digestion without aggravating Pitta. Chewed after meals.

name: Black Pepper
notes: Burns Ama (toxins). Enhances bioavailability of other herbs (like turmeric).
